basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Fāṭir — “the One who cleaves the origin into being” — opens with praise of the Originator of the heavens and the earth, who made the angels messengers with two, three or four wings, and who “adds to creation what He wills.” The sūra proclaims that no mercy God opens can be withheld, and none He withholds can be released. At its heart resounds the decisive call: “O mankind, you are the poor (al-fuqarāʾ) toward God, and God is the Rich, the Praiseworthy.” Qushayrī sees here the secret of the path: poverty of constitution is universal — every creature depends on its Creator to be and to last — while poverty of attribute is the divestment of the elite, until their faqr is pure. And this pearl: whoever grows poor in God grows rich through God; indigence toward Him is never without sufficiency through Him. The sūra then distinguishes the three ranks of the heirs of the Book — the one who wrongs himself, the moderate, the foremost in good — all united by the same gratuitous grace; it unfolds the signs of creation, the diversity of colors in fruits, mountains and people, to conclude: “only those of His servants who have knowledge truly fear God.” It is sealed on the merciful reprieve granted to mankind until the appointed term.
themes: the Originator and the angel messengers
the three ranks of the heirs of the Book
the fear proper to the knowers
the reprieve until the appointed term

versets.ar: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فَاطِرِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ جَاعِلِ الْمَلَائِكَةِ رُسُلًا أُولِي أَجْنِحَةٍ مَثْنَىٰ وَثُلَاثَ وَرُبَاعَ ۚ يَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ مَا يَشَاءُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: al-ḥamdu li-llāhi fāṭiri s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi jāʿili l-malāʾikati rusulan ulī ajniḥatin mathnā wa-thulātha wa-rubāʿa yazīdu fī l-khalqi mā yashāʾu inna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: Praise belongs to God, Originator of the heavens and the earth, who made the angels messengers with wings, two, three and four. He adds to creation what He wills. God has power over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: praise is His by right, for He alone holds the power to create the heavens and the earth. He has made Himself known to His servants through His acts and summoned them to ponder them: some are witnessed by sight — heavens, earth — others are grasped only by transmission and faith, not by reason; the angels are of these, whose form and manner of flight we cannot fathom. As for “He adds to creation what He wills,” it has been heard in countless ways: beauty of face, beauty of voice, fineness of discernment, eloquence, but also understanding granted by God, generosity, contentment with the decree, loftiness of aspiration, humility, chastity in poverty, a heart cleansed of all malice, knowledge of God without proof, the surge of longing toward Him, and that one seeks for oneself no rank in either world.

mots.racine: ف ط ر
mots.def: faṭara: to cleave, to bring forth from nothing; the primal Creator.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / م ل ك / ر س ل
ج ن ح
ز ي د / خ ل ق / ش ي أ
mots.sens: He adds to creation what He wills

versets.ar: مَّا يَفْتَحِ اللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِن رَّحْمَةٍ فَلَا مُمْسِكَ لَهَا ۖ وَمَا يُمْسِكْ فَلَا مُرْسِلَ لَهُ مِن بَعْدِهِ ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: mā yaftaḥi llāhu li-n-nāsi min raḥmatin fa-lā mumsika lahā wa-mā yumsik fa-lā mursila lahu min baʿdihi wa-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: Whatever mercy God opens for mankind, none can withhold; and whatever He withholds, none can release after Him. He is the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens the verse far beyond mere material provision: what the Real causes to enter the hearts of the knowers as lights of realization, no cloud veils it, no brilliance overpowers it. Likewise: the certainty He fixes in the hearts of His friends, none can wrest from them; and the gates of remembrance He seals upon the hearts of His adversaries, none but He can open. Ease and deprivation alike are His alone: what He brings near to hearts of facilitation, none withholds; what He keeps from them, none can make possible without Him.

mots.racine: ف ت ح
ر ح م
م س ك
ع ز ز / ح ك م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ هَلْ مِنْ خَالِقٍ غَيْرُ اللَّهِ يَرْزُقُكُم مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۚ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُؤْفَكُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu dhkurū niʿmata llāhi ʿalaykum hal min khāliqin ghayru llāhi yarzuqukum mina s-samāʾi wa-l-arḍi lā ilāha illā huwa fa-annā tuʾfakūna
versets.trad: O mankind, remember God's blessing upon you! Is there any creator other than God who provides for you from heaven and earth? There is no god but He; how then are you turned away?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two remembrances: he who remembers the blessing is a man of worship and receives an increase; he who remembers the Benefactor is a man of yearning and receives another increase. Yet how vast the gap: the first sees his gifts grow in both worlds, the second sees His meeting grow. He divides the blessing in two: what He has guarded us from and what He has bestowed on us; remembering what was averted calls for the endurance of protection, remembering what was given perfects the grace. To know there is no Provider but Him frees the heart: one no longer hangs one's hope on a creature, nor on one's own scheming, and rests in the contemplation of His decree.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ن ع م
خ ل ق / غ ي ر
mots.sens: is there a creator other than God

mots.racine: ر ز ق
أ ف ك
mots.def: ufika: to be turned aside, deluded.

versets.ar: وَإِن يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِّن قَبْلِكَ ۚ وَإِلَى اللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ
versets.tr: wa-in yukadhdhibūka fa-qad kudhdhibat rusulun min qablika wa-ilā llāhi turjaʿu l-umūru
versets.trad: If they call you a liar, messengers before you were also called liars; and to God all matters return.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees here a consolation and a balm for patience: when the Prophet knows that the messengers before him met the same reception, that they endured and God sufficed them, he treads their path, takes them as models, and as He sufficed them he knows He will suffice him too. He discerns an indication for the sages and the people of the heart facing those ignorant of this Way: the people of the realities live perpetually in the trial of insult, save by veiling their state from others' eyes.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب
ك ذ ب / ر س ل / ق ب ل
ر ج ع / أ م ر

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ ۖ فَلَا تَغُرَّنَّكُمُ الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَا يَغُرَّنَّكُم بِاللَّهِ الْغَرُورُ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu inna waʿda llāhi ḥaqqun fa-lā taghurrannakumu l-ḥayātu d-dunyā wa-lā yaghurrannakum bi-llāhi l-gharūru
versets.trad: O mankind, God's promise is true! Let not the life of this world delude you, and let not the great Deluder delude you concerning God.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the truth of the promise on several registers: true in all He has announced will come to pass — true the promise of Resurrection, true that of sufficing and protecting whoever obeys Him, true that of the generosity reserved in the hereafter for the obedient, true the remorse promised to the rebellious. And he draws out the conduct: whoever knows this stands ready for death, no longer gnawed by anxiety over his provision — God suffices him in that — then hastens to multiply obedience in trust of the promise, and guards against transgressions in fear of the threat.

mots.racine: و ع د / ح ق ق
غ ر ر / ح ي ي / د ن و
mots.sens: let not this world delude you

mots.racine: غ ر ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ فَاتَّخِذُوهُ عَدُوًّا ۚ إِنَّمَا يَدْعُو حِزْبَهُ لِيَكُونُوا مِنْ أَصْحَابِ السَّعِيرِ
versets.tr: inna sh-shayṭāna lakum ʿaduwwun fa-ttakhidhūhu ʿaduwwan innamā yadʿū ḥizbahu li-yakūnū min aṣḥābi s-saʿīri
versets.trad: Satan is an enemy to you, so take him as an enemy. He calls his party only that they may become companions of the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: enmity toward Satan means opposing him without let-up; for some fight him in word yet accompany him in deed. You will withstand his hostility only by unceasing recourse to the Lord — a recourse sincere only through the truthfulness of seeking aid. Satan never tires of warring against you: be not for an instant heedless of your Master, lest your enemy spring forth, for he is forever poised in ambush. Then comes the tender counterpart: Satan is your enemy, hate him and take him as a foe; and I am your Protector and your Beloved, so love Me and accept Me as Beloved.

mots.racine: ش ط ن / ع د و
mots.sens: Satan is an enemy to you

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ع د و
د ع و / ح ز ب
ص ح ب / س ع ر

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ ۖ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: alladhīna kafarū lahum ʿadhābun shadīdun wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti lahum maghfiratun wa-ajrun kabīrun
versets.trad: Those who disbelieve shall have a severe punishment, while those who believe and do righteous deeds shall have forgiveness and a great reward.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes, for the disbelievers, a hastened punishment and a deferred one: the hastened is the scattering of their hearts, the blinding of their inner sight, the insolence of their aspiration to the point of being content with an idol for a god; the other, that of the hereafter, no Muslim is unaware of its harshness. For the believers, forgiveness is the veil cast today over their faults — without it they would be exposed, without it they would perish. And the great reward: here below, the sweetness of worship, the permanence of knowledge, the increases of certainty in the heart and the privileged states; in the hereafter, the fulfilment of the wish and the attaining of what surpasses all hope.

mots.racine: ك ف ر
ع ذ ب / ش د د
غ ف ر
أ ج ر / ك ب ر

versets.ar: أَفَمَن زُيِّنَ لَهُ سُوءُ عَمَلِهِ فَرَآهُ حَسَنًا ۖ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ يُضِلُّ مَن يَشَاءُ وَيَهْدِي مَن يَشَاءُ ۖ فَلَا تَذْهَبْ نَفْسُكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَسَرَاتٍ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ بِمَا يَصْنَعُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-man zuyyina lahu sūʾu ʿamalihi fa-raʾāhu ḥasanan fa-inna llāha yuḍillu man yashāʾu wa-yahdī man yashāʾu fa-lā tadhhab nafsuka ʿalayhim ḥasarātin inna llāha ʿalīmun bi-mā yaṣnaʿūna
versets.trad: Is he whose evil deed is made to seem fair to him, so that he sees it as good, [like one rightly guided]? God leads astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. So let not your soul waste away in regrets over them; God knows well what they do.
versets.commentaire: For Qushayrī, the illusion of embellished evil targets not only the disbeliever: he who amasses the lawful and the unlawful, hoards the rubble of the world without a thought for its passing or his own departure before he can enjoy it; he who follows his passions and barters an eternal repose in Paradise for an hour; he who prefers to his Lord anything of the created — for all of them, their evil deed has been made fair until it appears beautiful, for they are distracted from the sweetness of intimate converse. And the consolation: once the truth of the decree is recognized, knowing they have fallen from God's regard, after calling them openly and lavishing counsel — their response is not yours to give: make of it neither grief nor torment for your heart.

mots.racine: ز ي ن / س و أ / ع م ل
mots.sens: his evil deed was made fair to him

mots.racine: ر أ ي / ح س ن
mots.sens: and he sees it as good

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ش ي أ
mots.sens: He leads astray whom He wills

mots.racine: ذ ه ب / ن ف س / ح س ر
mots.sens: let not your soul waste in regrets over them

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ الَّذِي أَرْسَلَ الرِّيَاحَ فَتُثِيرُ سَحَابًا فَسُقْنَاهُ إِلَىٰ بَلَدٍ مَّيِّتٍ فَأَحْيَيْنَا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ النُّشُورُ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu lladhī arsala r-riyāḥa fa-tuthīru saḥāban fa-suqnāhu ilā baladin mayyitin fa-aḥyaynā bihi l-arḍa baʿda mawtihā kadhālika n-nushūru
versets.trad: It is God who sends the winds, and they raise a cloud which We drive to a dead land, and thereby revive the earth after its death. Such is the Resurrection.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in the rain that revives the earth a parable of the heart: likewise, when God wills to revive a servant's heart, He first sends the winds of hope, which stir the buried longings; then He raises the clouds of agitation and the burning of emotion; then He pours down a rain that brings to bloom in the heart the flowers of expansion and the lights of the spirit, until life grows sweet to it and the subtleties of intimacy are perfected. The gradual reviving of nature speaks the patient work of grace within the soul.

mots.racine: ر س ل / ر و ح
ث و ر / س ح ب
ح ي ي / أ ر ض
mots.sens: We revive by it the earth

mots.racine: ن ش ر

versets.ar: مَن كَانَ يُرِيدُ الْعِزَّةَ فَلِلَّهِ الْعِزَّةُ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِلَيْهِ يَصْعَدُ الْكَلِمُ الطَّيِّبُ وَالْعَمَلُ الصَّالِحُ يَرْفَعُهُ ۚ وَالَّذِينَ يَمْكُرُونَ السَّيِّئَاتِ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ ۖ وَمَكْرُ أُولَٰئِكَ هُوَ يَبُورُ
versets.tr: man kāna yurīdu l-ʿizzata fa-li-llāhi l-ʿizzatu jamīʿan ilayhi yaṣʿadu l-kalimu ṭ-ṭayyibu wa-l-ʿamalu ṣ-ṣāliḥu yarfaʿuhu wa-lladhīna yamkurūna s-sayyiʾāti lahum ʿadhābun shadīdun wa-makru ulāʾika huwa yabūru
versets.trad: Whoever desires might — to God belongs all might. To Him ascends the good word, and the righteous deed He raises up. Those who plot evil deeds shall have a severe punishment, and the plotting of such will come to nothing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever seeks might through himself, let him know that all might belongs to God and no creature owns the least share of it; let him seek it, then, from God. The good word that ascends is the one springing from sound belief — the two attestations — in sincerity; its ascent is an ascent of acceptance, for God receives it. He multiplies its faces: the word conforming to the Sunna, the heart's word praising the Lord, the supplication for the believers, the word stripped bare for the Real with no share for the servant. And the plotters' scheme recoils upon them: what they suppose to be a good He overturns into trial.

mots.racine: ر و د / ع ز ز
ص ع د / ك ل م / ط ي ب
mots.sens: to Him ascends the good word

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح / ر ف ع
م ك ر / ب و ر
mots.sens: the plotting of those comes to nothing

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ خَلَقَكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَكُمْ أَزْوَاجًا ۚ وَمَا تَحْمِلُ مِنْ أُنثَىٰ وَلَا تَضَعُ إِلَّا بِعِلْمِهِ ۚ وَمَا يُعَمَّرُ مِن مُّعَمَّرٍ وَلَا يُنقَصُ مِنْ عُمُرِهِ إِلَّا فِي كِتَابٍ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu khalaqakum min turābin thumma min nuṭfatin thumma jaʿalakum azwājan wa-mā taḥmilu min unthā wa-lā taḍaʿu illā bi-ʿilmihi wa-mā yuʿammaru min muʿammarin wa-lā yunqaṣu min ʿumurihi illā fī kitābin inna dhālika ʿalā llāhi yasīrun
versets.trad: God created you from dust, then from a drop, then made you pairs. No female conceives or gives birth except with His knowledge. No one is granted long life, nor is any life shortened, but it is in a Book. That is easy for God.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He reminds them of their origin lest they grow proud of their state. Then this striking image of clay: what is drawn from mud changes quickly, its cohesion is fragile — yet it can be mended with water; its earth recomposes. So when the Real pours upon him the water of His bounty, He restores him after sins have broken him. And the argument for forgiveness: since nothing of their states escaped Him at the dawn of their creation — He who took no pause in creating one He knew would sin — He will take no pause in forgiving one He sees sinning.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ت ر ب
ن ط ف
ج ع ل / ز و ج
ك ت ب

versets.ar: وَمَا يَسْتَوِي الْبَحْرَانِ هَٰذَا عَذْبٌ فُرَاتٌ سَائِغٌ شَرَابُهُ وَهَٰذَا مِلْحٌ أُجَاجٌ ۖ وَمِن كُلٍّ تَأْكُلُونَ لَحْمًا طَرِيًّا وَتَسْتَخْرِجُونَ حِلْيَةً تَلْبَسُونَهَا ۖ وَتَرَى الْفُلْكَ فِيهِ مَوَاخِرَ لِتَبْتَغُوا مِن فَضْلِهِ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā yastawī l-baḥrāni hādhā ʿadhbun furātun sāʾighun sharābuhu wa-hādhā milḥun ujājun wa-min kullin taʾkulūna laḥman ṭariyyan wa-tastakhrijūna ḥilyatan talbasūnahā wa-tarā l-fulka fīhi mawākhira li-tabtaghū min faḍlihi wa-laʿallakum tashkurūna
versets.trad: The two seas are not alike: this one sweet, fresh, pleasant to drink, that one salty, bitter; yet from each you eat fresh flesh and extract ornaments you wear. And you see the ships ploughing through it, that you may seek of His bounty and that you may be grateful.
themes: the two states of the soul
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the two seas a mirror of two states: the two conditions are not equal: this one is turned toward God, busy obeying Him, firm in His knowledge; that one turns away from God, contracts before His worship, contests His apportioning. The one is a cause of union, the other a cause of estrangement. Then he distinguishes the moments: expansion (basṭ) and its possessor in joy, contraction (qabḍ) and its possessor in lament; separation and its possessor in servanthood, union and its possessor in the contemplation of Lordship. Yet from each sea one draws flesh and adornment: each, in his state, draws near to his Lord and adorns himself at His door.

mots.racine: س و ي / ب ح ر
mots.sens: the two seas are not alike
mots.def: istawā: to be equal; baḥr (dual baḥrāni): sea.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ف ر ت
م ل ح / أ ج ج
خ ر ج / ح ل ي

versets.ar: يُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فِي النَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ النَّهَارَ فِي اللَّيْلِ وَسَخَّرَ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ كُلٌّ يَجْرِي لِأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ اللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ ۚ وَالَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِ مَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِن قِطْمِيرٍ
versets.tr: yūliju l-layla fī n-nahāri wa-yūliju n-nahāra fī l-layli wa-sakhkhara sh-shamsa wa-l-qamara kullun yajrī li-ajalin musamman dhālikumu llāhu rabbukum lahu l-mulku wa-lladhīna tadʿūna min dūnihi mā yamlikūna min qiṭmīrin
versets.trad: He makes the night enter the day and the day enter the night, and has subjected the sun and the moon, each running for an appointed term. Such is God, your Lord; to Him belongs the dominion. Those you call upon besides Him do not own so much as the husk of a date-stone.
versets.commentaire: The alternation of night and day becomes for Qushayrī the inner rhythm: the lower soul prevails once over the heart, the heart once over the soul; so too with contraction and expansion, sobriety and intoxication, annihilation and subsistence — now one dominates, now the other. And He does likewise: He subjects the suns of oneness and the moons of knowledge as He wills to manifest them upon the hearts. Against this, the false gods do not own even the husk of a date-stone (qiṭmīr): show Me one particle, in denial or affirmation, of what you invoke besides Him!

mots.racine: و ل ج / ل ي ل / ن ه ر
mots.sens: He inserts the night into the day

mots.racine: س خ ر / ش م س / ق م ر
mots.sens: He subjected the sun and the moon

mots.racine: أ ج ل / س م و
mots.def: ajal musammā: a named, fixed term.

mots.racine: م ل ك / ق ط م ر
mots.def: qiṭmīr: the thin membrane on a date-stone.

versets.ar: إِن تَدْعُوهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُوا دُعَاءَكُمْ وَلَوْ سَمِعُوا مَا اسْتَجَابُوا لَكُمْ ۖ وَيَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ يَكْفُرُونَ بِشِرْكِكُمْ ۚ وَلَا يُنَبِّئُكَ مِثْلُ خَبِيرٍ
versets.tr: in tadʿūhum lā yasmaʿū duʿāʾakum wa-law samiʿū mā stajābū lakum wa-yawma l-qiyāmati yakfurūna bi-shirkikum wa-lā yunabbiʾuka mithlu khabīrin
versets.trad: If you call upon them, they do not hear your call, and were they to hear, they would not respond to you. On the Day of Resurrection they will disown your association of them. None can inform you like One who is All-Aware.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if you seek help from your idols, they do not help you; if you call upon them, they do not hear your call; and even if — by way of supposition — they heard, they would not answer you, for they hold no benefit for themselves: how then would they hold any for another? And on the Day of Resurrection: they will disown your association of them — they will believe only at that moment, but faith no longer avails once the obligation is lifted.

mots.racine: د ع و
س م ع / د ع و
mots.sens: they do not hear your call

mots.racine: ك ف ر / ش ر ك
م ث ل / خ ب ر

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ أَنتُمُ الْفُقَرَاءُ إِلَى اللَّهِ ۖ وَاللَّهُ هُوَ الْغَنِيُّ الْحَمِيدُ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu antumu l-fuqarāʾu ilā llāhi wa-llāhu huwa l-ghaniyyu l-ḥamīdu
versets.trad: O mankind, you are the poor toward God, and God is the Rich (al-Ghanī, the Self-Sufficient), the Praiseworthy.
versets.commentaire: This is the heart of the sūra, and Qushayrī unfolds here his science of faqr: poverty is of two kinds: poverty of constitution and poverty of attribute. The first is universal — every creature, brought forth from nothing, depends on its Creator to bring it into being, then to sustain and preserve it: God is Rich in His essence, the servant poor in his essence. The poverty of attribute is divestment: for the common, to divest oneself of material wealth; for the elite, to divest oneself of pretexts so that their poverty may be pure. And this pearl: whoever grows poor in God grows rich through God; the poor one turned toward God is the rich one in God, for indigence in Him is never without sufficiency through Him. Finally the delicacy of the Name: if He tells us “you are the poor,” it is not to demean us — His generosity is too holy for that — but so that to His word “God is the Rich, and you the poor” His bounty toward us may answer.

mots.racine: ف ق ر
mots.sens: you are the poor toward God

mots.racine: غ ن ي
ح م د

versets.ar: إِن يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ وَيَأْتِ بِخَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ
versets.tr: in yashaʾ yudhhibkum wa-yaʾti bi-khalqin jadīdin
versets.trad: If He willed, He could remove you and bring forth a new creation;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī links this verse to the preceding one as a hidden tenderness: He has let you know that He has no need of you, and shown you the place of your poverty toward Him, and that you cannot do without Him; yet the aim of all this is none but His will to honor you and shelter you under the wing of His favors. The reminder of His power to renew creation is not a threat but a loving admonition: your very dependence is the gate of His grace.

mots.racine: ش ي أ
ذ ه ب
خ ل ق / ج د د

versets.ar: وَمَا ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ بِعَزِيزٍ
versets.tr: wa-mā dhālika ʿalā llāhi bi-ʿazīzin
versets.trad: and that is no hard matter for God.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī continues the indication of verse 16: to replace one creation with another costs Him nothing. He has made known to you His independence of you and made you contemplate the place of your need of Him; thus, that nothing is hard for His power should return the servant not to servile fear but to confident surrender into the hands of the One for whom all is easy.

mots.racine: ع ز ز

versets.ar: وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ ۚ وَإِن تَدْعُ مُثْقَلَةٌ إِلَىٰ حِمْلِهَا لَا يُحْمَلْ مِنْهُ شَيْءٌ وَلَوْ كَانَ ذَا قُرْبَىٰ ۗ إِنَّمَا تُنذِرُ الَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُم بِالْغَيْبِ وَأَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ ۚ وَمَن تَزَكَّىٰ فَإِنَّمَا يَتَزَكَّىٰ لِنَفْسِهِ ۚ وَإِلَى اللَّهِ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-lā taziru wāziratun wizra ukhrā wa-in tadʿu muthqalatun ilā ḥimlihā lā yuḥmal minhu shayʾun wa-law kāna dhā qurbā innamā tundhiru lladhīna yakhshawna rabbahum bi-l-ghaybi wa-aqāmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-man tazakkā fa-innamā yatazakkā li-nafsihi wa-ilā llāhi l-maṣīru
versets.trad: No bearer of burdens shall bear another's burden; and if one heavily laden calls for its load to be carried, nothing of it will be borne, even by a near kinsman. You can only warn those who fear their Lord in the Unseen and establish the prayer. Whoever purifies himself purifies himself only for his own good; and to God is the journey's end.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: each is held to account for his own deed, each reckoned for his own register. Though some acts admit of substitution, in the realm of gnosis there is none: should a rebellious servant, sunk in his straying, miss an obligatory prayer — were a thousand saints and a thousand elect to perform it for him, each rakʿa worth a thousand, it would not be accepted from him; he must come himself. And warning works only on the heart already disposed: to warn is to point out the place of peril, and fear is dread; no dread profits except one who has fear — the birds of the sky alight according to their kind.

mots.racine: و ز ر
ث ق ل
خ ش ي / ر ب ب / غ ي ب
mots.sens: they fear their Lord in the Unseen
mots.def: khashiya: to fear with reverence; ghayb: the unseen.

mots.racine: ز ك و
mots.def: tazakkā: to purify, grow in purity.

versets.ar: وَمَا يَسْتَوِي الْأَعْمَىٰ وَالْبَصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-mā yastawī l-aʿmā wa-l-baṣīru
versets.trad: The blind and the seeing are not equal,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens a series of pairs that are never equal. Just as the blind and the seeing are not equal, he transposes: not equal is the one joined to Us and the one distracted from Us, the one drawn toward Us and the one veiled from Us. Blindness is not only of the eyes but of the heart cut off from the light of knowledge.

mots.racine: س و ي
ع م ي
ب ص ر

versets.ar: وَلَا الظُّلُمَاتُ وَلَا النُّورُ
versets.tr: wa-lā ẓ-ẓulumātu wa-lā n-nūru
versets.trad: nor the darknesses and the light,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī continues the litany of irreducible opposites: the darknesses are not equal to the light. Inwardly, the darknesses are those of the heart given over to heedlessness, the light that of the heart God causes to contemplate — the one He elected from eternity is not the one He rendered wretched by the prior decree.

mots.racine: ظ ل م
ن و ر

versets.ar: وَلَا الظِّلُّ وَلَا الْحَرُورُ
versets.tr: wa-lā ẓ-ẓillu wa-lā l-ḥarūru
versets.trad: nor the shade and the scorching heat;
versets.commentaire: Third pair of the series: cool shade is not equal to burning heat. Qushayrī hears in it the gap between the one grace shelters and the one delivered to the ardor of distance — not equal is the one We made witness to Our truth and the one whose heart We turned away from Our remembrance.

mots.racine: ظ ل ل
ح ر ر

versets.ar: وَمَا يَسْتَوِي الْأَحْيَاءُ وَلَا الْأَمْوَاتُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُسْمِعُ مَن يَشَاءُ ۖ وَمَا أَنتَ بِمُسْمِعٍ مَّن فِي الْقُبُورِ
versets.tr: wa-mā yastawī l-aḥyāʾu wa-lā l-amwātu inna llāha yusmiʿu man yashāʾu wa-mā anta bi-musmiʿin man fī l-qubūri
versets.trad: and the living and the dead are not equal. God makes hear whom He wills, but you cannot make those in the graves hear;
versets.commentaire: The culminating pair: the living and the dead. Qushayrī seals the series with a verse of love: our beloveds are divided — the faithful and the faithless — and never are the lover and the hater equal. To make hear is the work of God alone: you cannot make those in the graves hear — an image of dead hearts that no human word revives without grace.

mots.racine: ح ي ي
م و ت
س م ع / ش ي أ
mots.sens: He makes hear whom He wills

mots.racine: ق ب ر

versets.ar: إِنْ أَنتَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ
versets.tr: in anta illā nadhīrun
versets.trad: you are but a warner.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī returns the Prophet to his pure function: you are but a warner — the guidance of hearts is not in your power, but in God's alone. The greatness of the mission lies precisely in this effacement: to convey and to warn, without arrogating what belongs only to the Real, the steering of souls.

mots.racine: ن ذ ر

versets.ar: إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ بِالْحَقِّ بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا ۚ وَإِن مِّنْ أُمَّةٍ إِلَّا خَلَا فِيهَا نَذِيرٌ
versets.tr: innā arsalnāka bi-l-ḥaqqi bashīran wa-nadhīran wa-in min ummatin illā khalā fīhā nadhīrun
versets.trad: We have sent you with the truth as a bringer of good news and a warner. There is no community but a warner has passed among it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: no community among those before you was left without a warner We raised up among them; and in your time We sent you to all the nations together, with the truth. The verse attests that no age, no people was left without a Law and a call — yet the Prophet receives this privilege: to be the sole one sent to the totality of creatures.

mots.racine: ر س ل / ح ق ق
mots.sens: We sent you with the truth

mots.racine: ب ش ر / ن ذ ر
mots.sens: a bringer of good news and a warner

mots.racine: خ ل و / ن ذ ر
mots.sens: but a warner passed among it
mots.def: khalā: to have passed, to have been present.

versets.ar: وَإِن يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ جَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ وَبِالزُّبُرِ وَبِالْكِتَابِ الْمُنِيرِ
versets.tr: wa-in yukadhdhibūka fa-qad kadhdhaba lladhīna min qablihim jāʾathum rusuluhum bi-l-bayyināti wa-bi-z-zuburi wa-bi-l-kitābi l-munīri
versets.trad: If they call you a liar, those before them also denied; their messengers came to them with clear proofs, the scriptures, and the illuminating Book.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if they meet you with denial, such is their custom with every prophet; and if they persist in the way of error, you will find no change in God's custom of punishment and humiliation. Rejection is no verdict on the truth of the message but the repetition of an ancient stance, to which the divine sanction responds, unchanging.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / ق ب ل
ب ي ن
ز ب ر
ك ت ب / ن و ر

versets.ar: ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ۖ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ
versets.tr: thumma akhadhtu lladhīna kafarū fa-kayfa kāna nakīri
versets.trad: Then I seized those who disbelieved; and how [terrible] was My reproof!
versets.commentaire: After the denial comes the seizing. Qushayrī makes the fate of the ancient deniers a mirror held up to the present: God's custom admits of no change; those who persist in blindness meet the same reproof (nakīr). The rhetorical question “how was My reproof!” conveys the severity of the divine reversal upon the obstinate.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ
ك ف ر
ن ك ر

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ أَنزَلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِ ثَمَرَاتٍ مُّخْتَلِفًا أَلْوَانُهَا ۚ وَمِنَ الْجِبَالِ جُدَدٌ بِيضٌ وَحُمْرٌ مُّخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَانُهَا وَغَرَابِيبُ سُودٌ
versets.tr: a-lam tara anna llāha anzala mina s-samāʾi māʾan fa-akhrajnā bihi thamarātin mukhtalifan alwānuhā wa-mina l-jibāli judadun bīḍun wa-ḥumrun mukhtalifun alwānuhā wa-gharābību sūdun
versets.trad: Have you not seen that God sends down water from the sky, and We bring forth thereby fruits of varied colors; and in the mountains are streaks white and red, of varied hues, and intensely black?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He has shown, in this verse and its like, that the specification of the work by its forms and colors is a proof of the Agent's intention and His argument; and that the perfection of the work, its completion, testifies to the Maker's knowledge and makes it known. The dazzling diversity of fruits and mountains is no accident but a signature: it speaks a design, hence an Author.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / س م و / م و ه
mots.sens: He sent down from the sky water

mots.racine: ث م ر / خ ل ف / ل و ن
ج د د / ب ي ض / ح م ر
غ ر ب / س و د

versets.ar: وَمِنَ النَّاسِ وَالدَّوَابِّ وَالْأَنْعَامِ مُخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَانُهُ كَذَٰلِكَ ۗ إِنَّمَا يَخْشَى اللَّهَ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ الْعُلَمَاءُ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ غَفُورٌ
versets.tr: wa-mina n-nāsi wa-d-dawābbi wa-l-anʿāmi mukhtalifun alwānuhu kadhālika innamā yakhshā llāha min ʿibādihi l-ʿulamāʾu inna llāha ʿazīzun ghafūrun
versets.trad: And of people, beasts, and cattle there are likewise varied colors. Only those of His servants who have knowledge truly fear God. Indeed, God is Mighty, Forgiving.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “only” (innamā) is a word of restriction and reservation: whoever lacks knowledge of God has no fear of God. He finely distinguishes fear (khashya) from dread (rahba): dread is a fear that makes its subject flee; fear, the moment it arises, reins in its subject's impulse and keeps him with God. And he multiplies its sources: the fear of the knowers comes from their sense of having fallen short of His due, from their shame before the Real's gaze, from the apprehension of committing an impropriety or a slackening. True knowledge does not swell: it inclines the heart to tremble with love.

mots.racine: د ب ب / ن ع م
mots.def: dābba: a moving creature; anʿām: livestock.

mots.racine: خ ش ي
ع ل م
mots.def: ʿālim (pl. ʿulamāʾ): one who knows God.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / غ ف ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَتْلُونَ كِتَابَ اللَّهِ وَأَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَأَنفَقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ سِرًّا وَعَلَانِيَةً يَرْجُونَ تِجَارَةً لَّن تَبُورَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yatlūna kitāba llāhi wa-aqāmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-anfaqū mimmā razaqnāhum sirran wa-ʿalāniyatan yarjūna tijāratan lan tabūra
versets.trad: Those who recite the Book of God, establish the prayer, and spend secretly and openly out of what We have provided them, hope for a commerce that will never perish:
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those whose every moment is absorbed in the remembrance of God and the fulfilment of His due, in the kinds of worship and the modes of nearness — to them the highest measure of proximity and the fullest portion of welcome. These are the honored saints. And he sets the contrast: those whose state is the reverse receive the reverse. The commerce that never perishes (tijāra lan tabūr) is that transaction in which the soul exchanges the ephemeral for the abiding meeting.

mots.racine: ت ل و / ك ت ب
mots.sens: they recite the Book of God

mots.racine: ن ف ق / ر ز ق
mots.sens: they spend of what We provided them

mots.racine: س ر ر / ع ل ن
ت ج ر / ب و ر

versets.ar: لِيُوَفِّيَهُمْ أُجُورَهُمْ وَيَزِيدَهُم مِّن فَضْلِهِ ۚ إِنَّهُ غَفُورٌ شَكُورٌ
versets.tr: li-yuwaffiyahum ujūrahum wa-yazīdahum min faḍlihi innahu ghafūrun shakūrun
versets.trad: that He may pay them their rewards in full and increase them from His bounty. Indeed, He is Forgiving, Appreciative.
versets.commentaire: The commerce of the preceding verse here finds its term: God pays the wages in full and adds of His bounty. Qushayrī underscores the two Names sealing the passage: Forgiving for the servant's shortcomings, Appreciative (shakūr) for the little he offers — for God accepts the smallest deed and rewards it beyond its measure. God's gratitude toward the servant is the height of generosity: He thanks whoever serves Him.

mots.racine: و ف ي / أ ج ر
mots.sens: to pay them their wages in full

mots.racine: ز ي د / ف ض ل
mots.sens: and increase them from His bounty

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ش ك ر
mots.def: shakūr: who accepts and rewards generously.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِي أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ هُوَ الْحَقُّ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بِعِبَادِهِ لَخَبِيرٌ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhī awḥaynā ilayka mina l-kitābi huwa l-ḥaqqu muṣaddiqan li-mā bayna yadayhi inna llāha bi-ʿibādihi la-khabīrun baṣīrun
versets.trad: What We have revealed to you of the Book is the truth, confirming what came before it. Indeed, God is All-Aware and All-Seeing of His servants.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī hears this verse as a confidence of election: what We have made known to you — Our choosing of you, Our predilection, your advancement above every creature — is in accord with what We have announced to you. And he cites this verse of love: “I desire no other than you for a friend: trust my word, for the noble are trustworthy.” The truth of the Book is doubled by a truth of the bond: God, perfectly aware of His servants, distinguishes the one He has chosen.

mots.racine: و ح ي
ح ق ق
ص د ق / ي د ي
خ ب ر / ب ص ر

versets.ar: ثُمَّ أَوْرَثْنَا الْكِتَابَ الَّذِينَ اصْطَفَيْنَا مِنْ عِبَادِنَا ۖ فَمِنْهُمْ ظَالِمٌ لِّنَفْسِهِ وَمِنْهُم مُّقْتَصِدٌ وَمِنْهُمْ سَابِقٌ بِالْخَيْرَاتِ بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الْفَضْلُ الْكَبِيرُ
versets.tr: thumma awrathnā l-kitāba lladhīna ṣṭafaynā min ʿibādinā fa-minhum ẓālimun li-nafsihi wa-minhum muqtaṣidun wa-minhum sābiqun bi-l-khayrāti bi-idhni llāhi dhālika huwa l-faḍlu l-kabīru
versets.trad: Then We bequeathed the Book to those We chose of Our servants. Among them is one who wrongs himself, one who is moderate, and one who is foremost in good deeds by God's leave: that is the great bounty,
themes: the three ranks of the heirs
versets.commentaire: This is one of the sūra's summits, and Qushayrī unfolds countless readings of the three ranks. A subtlety of language: to the “wrongdoer against himself” is attached the phrase “against himself,” and to the “foremost” the phrase “by God's leave”: the wrongdoer raised up his fault by “against himself,” the foremost broke his pride by “by God's leave.” Hence this inner dialogue: “O wrongdoer, lift your head: you have sinned, but against yourself. O foremost, lower your head: you have outstripped, but by God's leave.” And the pearl of mercy: the All-Mighty, if He sees a wrongdoer, crushes him; but the Generous, if He sees one wronged, takes him by the hand — He looks upon the wrongdoer as one wronged, wronged by his own soul that drove him to wrong another. And this is why “that is the great bounty”: because He mentioned the wrongdoer alongside the foremost.

mots.racine: و ر ث / ك ت ب
mots.def: awratha: to pass on as inheritance.

mots.racine: ص ف و / ع ب د
ظ ل م / ن ف س
ق ص د
mots.def: muqtaṣid: who keeps the middle course.

mots.racine: س ب ق / خ ي ر
mots.def: sābiq: who precedes; khayrāt: good works.

versets.ar: جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ يَدْخُلُونَهَا يُحَلَّوْنَ فِيهَا مِنْ أَسَاوِرَ مِن ذَهَبٍ وَلُؤْلُؤًا ۖ وَلِبَاسُهُمْ فِيهَا حَرِيرٌ
versets.tr: jannātu ʿadnin yadkhulūnahā yuḥallawna fīhā min asāwira min dhahabin wa-luʾluʾan wa-libāsuhum fīhā ḥarīrun
versets.trad: Gardens of Eden which they shall enter, adorned therein with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their garment therein is silk.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī warns that entry into the Garden is never a due: He has signified that their entry into Paradise is not by merit but by His grace; and in grace there is no distinction of ranks. The bracelets, the pearls, the silk are marks of an honor received, not earned — which unites the wrongdoer, the moderate, and the foremost in one and the same gratuitous bounty.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ع د ن
mots.def: jannāt ʿadn: gardens of perpetual abode.

mots.racine: ح ل ي
س و ر / ذ ه ب
ل ب س / ح ر ر

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَذْهَبَ عَنَّا الْحَزَنَ ۖ إِنَّ رَبَّنَا لَغَفُورٌ شَكُورٌ
versets.tr: wa-qālū l-ḥamdu li-llāhi lladhī adhhaba ʿannā l-ḥazana inna rabbanā la-ghafūrun shakūrun
versets.trad: And they will say: “Praise belongs to God who has removed all sorrow from us! Truly our Lord is Forgiving, Appreciative,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they have been realized in the truths of contentment. Sorrow (ḥazan) is so named for the roughness (ḥuzūna) of the moment upon the one who suffers it; yet in Paradise there is no roughness: only contentment and rejoicing. He probes its origin: that sorrow was the sorrow of fearing the outcome, the constant vigilance lest one fail in propriety, the disciplining of the soul. And he notes the delicacy in the order of the Names: “Forgiving” for the rebellious, “Appreciative” for the obedient — He placed first what belongs to the rebellious, out of gentleness toward them, because of the weakness of their state.

mots.racine: ذ ه ب / ح ز ن
غ ف ر
ش ك ر

versets.ar: الَّذِي أَحَلَّنَا دَارَ الْمُقَامَةِ مِن فَضْلِهِ لَا يَمَسُّنَا فِيهَا نَصَبٌ وَلَا يَمَسُّنَا فِيهَا لُغُوبٌ
versets.tr: alladhī aḥallanā dāra l-muqāmati min faḍlihi lā yamassunā fīhā naṣabun wa-lā yamassunā fīhā lughūbun
versets.trad: who has settled us, by His grace, in the abode of permanence, where no fatigue touches us and no weariness afflicts us.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “the abode of permanence” (dār al-muqāma): the abode of abiding, from which they desire no removal, for which they wish no exit. And he attaches to it his doctrine of vision: when they wish to see their Master, they have no distance to cross: in their dwellings they receive greeting and peace; and when they see Him, they need turn neither eye nor pupil toward any direction — they see Him as they are, without modality. No fatigue, no weariness: effort itself is abolished in permanence.

mots.racine: ح ل ل / د و ر / ق و م
mots.sens: He settled us in the abode of abiding

mots.racine: ف ض ل
م س س / ن ص ب
ل غ ب

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَهُمْ نَارُ جَهَنَّمَ لَا يُقْضَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ فَيَمُوتُوا وَلَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُم مِّنْ عَذَابِهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِي كُلَّ كَفُورٍ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kafarū lahum nāru jahannama lā yuqḍā ʿalayhim fa-yamūtū wa-lā yukhaffafu ʿanhum min ʿadhābihā kadhālika najzī kulla kafūrin
versets.trad: But those who disbelieve shall have the fire of Hell; no decree of death is passed upon them that they might die, nor is any of its punishment lightened for them. Thus do We requite every ingrate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sums up the deniers' fate in a tight formula: no life they enjoy, no death in which they find rest; they remain in punishment and veiling, the torment never slackening, the penalty never lifted. The worst of the fire is not only the burning but the veil (ḥijāb) — the lasting privation of the Face, which redoubles the suffering of absence.

mots.racine: ن و ر / ج ه ن م
mots.sens: theirs is the fire of Hell

mots.racine: ق ض ي / م و ت
mots.sens: it is not decreed that they die

mots.racine: خ ف ف
mots.sens: nor is it lightened for them

mots.racine: ك ف ر

versets.ar: وَهُمْ يَصْطَرِخُونَ فِيهَا رَبَّنَا أَخْرِجْنَا نَعْمَلْ صَالِحًا غَيْرَ الَّذِي كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ ۚ أَوَلَمْ نُعَمِّرْكُم مَّا يَتَذَكَّرُ فِيهِ مَن تَذَكَّرَ وَجَاءَكُمُ النَّذِيرُ ۖ فَذُوقُوا فَمَا لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِن نَّصِيرٍ
versets.tr: wa-hum yaṣṭarikhūna fīhā rabbanā akhrijnā naʿmal ṣāliḥan ghayra lladhī kunnā naʿmalu a-wa-lam nuʿammirkum mā yatadhakkaru fīhi man tadhakkara wa-jāʾakumu n-nadhīru fa-dhūqū fa-mā li-ẓ-ẓālimīna min naṣīrin
versets.trad: And they will cry out therein: “Our Lord, bring us out! We will do righteousness, other than what we used to do!” Did We not give you a life long enough for whoever would reflect to reflect? And the warner came to you. So taste! For the wrongdoers have no helper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes audible the divine reply to the cry of the damned: they say “Lord, bring us out, we will do good differently”; they are told: did We not grant you a lifetime? Did the warner not come to you before the age of grey hair? And he glosses the warner in several striking ways: the messengers — but also the frailty of old age, the falling of teeth, the bending of the back. Life itself, in its signs of decline, is a silent warner one refused to hear.

mots.racine: ص ر خ
ر ب ب / خ ر ج
ع م ر
mots.sens: did We not give you long life

mots.racine: ذ و ق

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَالِمُ غَيْبِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۚ إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ
versets.tr: inna llāha ʿālimu ghaybi s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi innahu ʿalīmun bi-dhāti ṣ-ṣudūri
versets.trad: Indeed, God is Knower of the unseen of the heavens and the earth. He knows well what is within the breasts.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the object of this hidden knowledge: He knows the sincerity of the sincere, the truthfulness of the truthful, the hypocrisy of the hypocrites, the denial of the deniers; He knows who wishes ill upon people and who thinks of God a beautiful thought. God's knowledge is not neutral: it distinguishes the states of the heart, and herein lies its mercy as much as its rigor — nothing of the secret intention escapes Him.

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب / س م و
mots.sens: Knower of the unseen of the heavens

mots.racine: ع ل م / ص د ر
mots.sens: Knower of what is in the breasts
mots.def: dhāt aṣ-ṣudūr: the inmost content of hearts.

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَكُمْ خَلَائِفَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ فَمَن كَفَرَ فَعَلَيْهِ كُفْرُهُ ۖ وَلَا يَزِيدُ الْكَافِرِينَ كُفْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا مَقْتًا ۖ وَلَا يَزِيدُ الْكَافِرِينَ كُفْرُهُمْ إِلَّا خَسَارًا
versets.tr: huwa lladhī jaʿalakum khalāʾifa fī l-arḍi fa-man kafara fa-ʿalayhi kufruhu wa-lā yazīdu l-kāfirīna kufruhum ʿinda rabbihim illā maqtan wa-lā yazīdu l-kāfirīna kufruhum illā khasāran
versets.trad: It is He who made you successors on the earth. Whoever disbelieves, his disbelief is upon him. For the disbelievers, their disbelief only increases the loathing of their Lord; for the disbelievers, their disbelief only increases their loss.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates on the succession of generations: the people of each age succeed those before them. Some bear the burden of their predecessors; others are base and vile. The people of merit are a trial for their time; the vile are themselves the trial of their time. Stewardship (khilāfa) is thus a trust that reveals what one is: an honor for the one who shoulders it, an increase of loss for the one who betrays it by denial.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / خ ل ف / أ ر ض
mots.sens: He made you successors on the earth

mots.racine: ك ف ر
م ق ت
خ س ر

versets.ar: قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ شُرَكَاءَكُمُ الَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ أَرُونِي مَاذَا خَلَقُوا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ أَمْ لَهُمْ شِرْكٌ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ أَمْ آتَيْنَاهُمْ كِتَابًا فَهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَتٍ مِّنْهُ ۚ بَلْ إِن يَعِدُ الظَّالِمُونَ بَعْضُهُم بَعْضًا إِلَّا غُرُورًا
versets.tr: qul a-raʾaytum shurakāʾakumu lladhīna tadʿūna min dūni llāhi arūnī mādhā khalaqū mina l-arḍi am lahum shirkun fī s-samāwāti am ātaynāhum kitāban fa-hum ʿalā bayyinatin minhu bal in yaʿidu ẓ-ẓālimūna baʿḍuhum baʿḍan illā ghurūran
versets.trad: Say: “Have you considered your partners whom you call upon besides God? Show me what they have created of the earth! Or do they have a share in the heavens? Or have We given them a Book so they stand on a clear proof from it?” No! The wrongdoers promise one another nothing but delusion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He repeats the exposure of their idols' impotence and the deficiency of those they took as gods among their statues, so as to make foolish their opinions and alert them to the baseness of their states, their acts, their aspirations and the defect of their reason. Then He announces that they will produce nothing of what is demanded of them: no sound answer to the question posed — their mutual promises are mere mirage.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / ش ر ك
ر أ ي / خ ل ق
mots.sens: show me what they have created
mots.def: arā: to make see, to show.

mots.racine: ش ر ك / س م و
mots.sens: or have they a share in the heavens

mots.racine: غ ر ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُمْسِكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ أَن تَزُولَا ۚ وَلَئِن زَالَتَا إِنْ أَمْسَكَهُمَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِّن بَعْدِهِ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ حَلِيمًا غَفُورًا
versets.tr: inna llāha yumsiku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa an tazūlā wa-la-in zālatā in amsakahumā min aḥadin min baʿdihi innahu kāna ḥalīman ghafūran
versets.trad: Indeed, God holds the heavens and the earth lest they cease. And were they to cease, none could hold them after Him. Indeed, He is Forbearing, Forgiving.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gathers in a cascade the work of the Holding: He holds them by His power, perfected them by His wisdom, ordered them by His will, and created their inhabitants according to His decree; no partner shares with Him their holding nor their annihilation, no associate in their existence nor their ordering. The cosmos suspended without visible support is the vastest sign of the Agent's oneness: all rests upon Him alone.

mots.racine: م س ك / س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: He holds the heavens and the earth

mots.racine: ز و ل
ح ل م / غ ف ر

versets.ar: وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَانِهِمْ لَئِن جَاءَهُمْ نَذِيرٌ لَّيَكُونُنَّ أَهْدَىٰ مِنْ إِحْدَى الْأُمَمِ ۖ فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ نَذِيرٌ مَّا زَادَهُمْ إِلَّا نُفُورًا
versets.tr: wa-aqsamū bi-llāhi jahda aymānihim la-in jāʾahum nadhīrun la-yakūnunna ahdā min iḥdā l-umami fa-lammā jāʾahum nadhīrun mā zādahum illā nufūran
versets.trad: They swore by God their most solemn oaths that if a warner came to them, they would be better guided than any of the nations. Yet when a warner came to them, it only increased their aversion,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns this broken oath into a mirror of the inconstant disciple: their words have no reality, their pledges no guarantee; what they promise of themselves is sheer falsehood, their displayed fidelity sheer deceit. So the murīd, in the time of his fervor, boasts before his elder of having made a pact with God, of having sealed his covenant — but when his passion bites him and Satan seeks to belie him, it fells him by its cunning and casts him into the pit of his straying; then his face blackens and his standing fades with God.

mots.racine: ق س م
ج ه د / ي م ن
ك و ن / ه د ي
ز ي د / ن ف ر

versets.ar: اسْتِكْبَارًا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَمَكْرَ السَّيِّئِ ۚ وَلَا يَحِيقُ الْمَكْرُ السَّيِّئُ إِلَّا بِأَهْلِهِ ۚ فَهَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا سُنَّتَ الْأَوَّلِينَ ۚ فَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّتِ اللَّهِ تَبْدِيلًا ۖ وَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّتِ اللَّهِ تَحْوِيلًا
versets.tr: istikbāran fī l-arḍi wa-makra s-sayyiʾi wa-lā yaḥīqu l-makru s-sayyiʾu illā bi-ahlihi fa-hal yanẓurūna illā sunnata l-awwalīna fa-lan tajida li-sunnati llāhi tabdīlan wa-lan tajida li-sunnati llāhi taḥwīlan
versets.trad: in arrogance on the earth and in evil scheming. But evil scheming engulfs only its own people. Do they then await anything but the way of the ancients? You will find no change in God's way, and you will find no turning of God's way.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: in sum, no friend of God has ever been let down, no enemy has ever profited. Whoever turns his design against the Truth does not reach the reality: his scheme recoils upon him. The divine sunna is without change or turning: He shatters His adversaries in an annihilation, and unfurls for His friends an immense grace. The wicked plot is a taut rope that binds only the one who knots it.

mots.racine: ك ب ر / أ ر ض
م ك ر / س و أ
ح ي ق / أ ه ل
mots.def: ḥāqa: to encompass, to recoil upon.

mots.racine: س ن ن / ب د ل

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ وَكَانُوا أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً ۚ وَمَا كَانَ اللَّهُ لِيُعْجِزَهُ مِن شَيْءٍ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَلَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ عَلِيمًا قَدِيرًا
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yasīrū fī l-arḍi fa-yanẓurū kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu lladhīna min qablihim wa-kānū ashadda minhum quwwatan wa-mā kāna llāhu li-yuʿjizahu min shayʾin fī s-samāwāti wa-lā fī l-arḍi innahu kāna ʿalīman qadīran
versets.trad: Have they not journeyed through the land and seen what was the end of those before them, who were greater than them in strength? Nothing in the heavens or the earth can thwart God. Indeed, He is All-Knowing, All-Powerful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses the law of destinies: no friend of God has ever been let down, no enemy has ever profited; whoever's intention recoils does not touch the reality, his stratagem returns upon him. And He — glory to Him — annihilates His enemies in utter destruction, and widens for His friends an immense grace. To consider the end of the mighty peoples of old is to read in the open sky that nothing, nowhere, can reduce God to impotence.

mots.racine: س ي ر / أ ر ض
mots.sens: have they not journeyed through the land

mots.racine: ع ق ب / ق ب ل
mots.sens: the end of those before them

mots.racine: ش د د / ق و ي
ع ج ز / ش ي أ

versets.ar: وَلَوْ يُؤَاخِذُ اللَّهُ النَّاسَ بِمَا كَسَبُوا مَا تَرَكَ عَلَىٰ ظَهْرِهَا مِن دَابَّةٍ وَلَٰكِن يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۖ فَإِذَا جَاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ بِعِبَادِهِ بَصِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-law yuʾākhidhu llāhu n-nāsa bi-mā kasabū mā taraka ʿalā ẓahrihā min dābbatin wa-lākin yuʾakhkhiruhum ilā ajalin musamman fa-idhā jāʾa ajaluhum fa-inna llāha kāna bi-ʿibādihi baṣīran
versets.trad: If God were to take mankind to task for what they have earned, He would not leave a single creature on its surface; but He defers them to an appointed term. And when their term comes, indeed God is ever Seeing of His servants.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī seals the sūra on the wisdom of the deferral: if He hastened for them the reward or punishment they merit, their brief lives would not suffice for it, their short days would not have the breadth to contain it; so He has postponed it to the Day of Gathering — for that day is long. And the seal: God has power over all things, perfectly aware and all-seeing of the affairs of His servants. The reprieve is no forgetting but a patient mercy, beneath the gaze that sees all.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ
mots.sens: if God took mankind to task
mots.def: ʾākhadha: to call to account, to punish.

mots.racine: ك س ب
أ خ ر / أ ج ل / س م و
mots.sens: He defers them to an appointed term
mots.def: akhkhara: to postpone; ajal musammā: a named term.

mots.racine: ع ب د / ب ص ر
